text.title: Video Gen Basics — Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every short-clip AI video generation call must enforce. Source: Runway docs, Luma keyframe API, ffprobe behaviour, content-policy traces.

rule.statement: Every video prompt MUST specify camera movement from the closed vocabulary: `static | pan_left | pan_right | dolly_in | dolly_out | tracking | crane | drone | orbit`. Empty camera defaults reject at validation.

rationale.source: Runway and Luma comparative trials (10 clips per vocab term)

rule.rationale: Omitting camera lets the model choose randomly — output ranges from steady tracking to handheld shake across identical prompts. Explicit movement is the single largest quality lever.

rule.statement: When the brief carries `subject_consistency_required == true`, use image-to-video (Runway image_to_video, Luma keyframes.frame0, Replicate SVD). Text-to-video is allowed only for one-off prototypes.

rationale.source: Subject drift measurements across 20+ text-to-video runs

rule.rationale: Text-to-video regenerates the subject from scratch on every call — faces, brands, and lighting drift. Image-to-video anchors to a reference frame, eliminating drift across shots.

rule.statement: Run ffprobe on every generated video before returning success. Verify file size > 0, duration > 0, and at least one valid video stream. Empty / 0-byte outputs from SUCCEEDED responses are not infrequent.
rule.rationale: Provider can return status SUCCEEDED with a 0-byte URL or a corrupted file. Propagating these silently breaks downstream ffmpeg processing with cryptic errors hours later.

rule.statement: Cache generated clips by `sha256(prompt_text + provider + seed + duration + aspect_ratio).hexdigest()`. Store seed alongside the clip; pass it back for near-identical regeneration.
rule.rationale: Iteration loops without cache+seed burn budget on identical prompts. With seed stored, minor prompt tweaks regenerate predictably and cheap re-runs become free.

rule.statement: Cap individual clip duration at 5 seconds (Luma native, Runway optimal) or 10 seconds (Runway max). For longer sequences, generate multiple ≤5s clips and concatenate via ffmpeg concat.

rationale.source: Quality degradation curve on Runway Gen-3 (motion artifacts above 8s)

rule.rationale: Longer clips accumulate motion artifacts (warp, blur, identity drift). Concatenating ≤5s segments preserves quality at the cost of visible seams; seam mitigation is post-processing, not generation.

rule.statement: Agents MUST NOT attempt to read or stream video file content inline as part of an agent response. Download to disk first, return only the file path; downstream processing (ffprobe, ffmpeg) reads from path.

rationale.source: Token budget: a 5s 1080p mp3 is ~5-15 MB binary

rule.rationale: Video files are large binary blobs. Inline read either truncates at the binary boundary or blows the context window. The path-only contract keeps agents inside their token budget.
